name: eval-teammates
description: jaxaht/eval-teammates dataset hosted on Hugging Face and contributed by the HF Datasets community
rai:dataLimitations: This dataset contains evaluation teammate policies for three cooperative environments: Level-Based Foraging (LBF; lbf_7x7_nolevels and lbf_12x12), Overcooked-v1 (cramped_room, asymm_advantages, coord_ring, counter_circuit, forced_coord), and Mini-Hanabi (2-player). Teammates are generated by a fixed set of algorithmic methods (FCP, BRDiv, LBRDiv, CoMeDi, IPPO, and rule-based heuristics); the distribution may not represent the full range of possible cooperative strategies or human behavior. Overcooked layouts are limited to the five maps from Carroll et al. (2019). Mini-Hanabi coverage is currently limited to 2-player settings. Generalization to other cooperative environments or task configurations has not been established.
rai:dataBiases: The RL-trained evaluation teammates (FCP, BRDiv, LBRDiv, CoMeDi, IPPO) are biased toward strategies that emerge from cooperative self-play or diversity-maximizing objectives, which may not reflect human collaborative behavior. The Hanabi behavioral cloning agent is trained on the AH2AC2 human dataset (Dizdarević et al. 2025), which covers games played by participants recruited for that study and may not represent the full distribution of human Hanabi strategies. Rule-based Hanabi agents (e.g., IGGI, SmartBot, PIERS) encode specific conventions that may not generalize across partner types. No demographic information about human participants involved in upstream data collection is available.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. All agents are computationally generated policies (neural network weights or rule-based programs). The Hanabi BC-LSTM agent is trained on anonymized game logs with no personally identifiable information.
rai:dataUseCases: This dataset is intended to serve as a held-out evaluation set for benchmarking ad hoc teamwork (AHT) ego agents across LBF, Overcooked-v1, and Mini-Hanabi tasks. Validity has been established for: (1) zero-shot cross-play evaluation of ego agents against held-out teammates, and (2) computing cross-play matrices. This dataset is not intended for use as training partners (use training-time teammate generation methods instead), as a human behavior model, or for deployment in real human-AI interaction without further validation.
rai:dataSocialImpact: This dataset is a research benchmark artifact enabling standardized, reproducible evaluation of ad hoc teamwork methods in cooperative multi-agent settings. Positive impacts include lowering barriers to fair comparison across AHT research and supporting progress toward robust human-AI collaboration. Potential negative impacts include over-reliance on these specific environments as proxies for human-AI cooperation, and the risk that methods optimized for this benchmark may not transfer to real human partners.

distribution.name: repo
distribution.description: The Hugging Face git repository containing evaluation teammate agent checkpoints.

distribution.name: checkpoint-metadata-files
distribution.description: Sentinel _CHECKPOINT_METADATA files marking each evaluation teammate checkpoint, organized by environment and agent.

recordSet.name: checkpoints
recordSet.description: One record per evaluation teammate checkpoint. Environment, agent, and training timestamp are extracted from the checkpoint file path.

field.name: checkpoints/environment
field.description: The cooperative environment for which this teammate was trained (e.g. lbf_7x7_nolevels, overcooked_v1_coord_ring).

field.name: checkpoints/agent
field.description: The evaluation teammate identifier (e.g. fcp_s0, brdiv_conf1_m0, ippo_mlp_s2c0).

field.name: checkpoints/checkpoint_path
field.description: Full relative path to the _CHECKPOINT_METADATA file within the repository.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: Overcooked-AI (Carroll et al. 2019)
prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: The five Overcooked-v1 task layouts used in this dataset (cramped_room, asymm_advantages, coord_ring, counter_circuit, forced_coord) were introduced by Carroll et al. (2019). Human-human and human-AI gameplay data collected by Carroll et al. informed the design of these layouts as benchmarks for human-AI coordination.
prov:wasDerivedFrom.citation: Carroll, M., Shah, R., Ho, M. K., Griffiths, T. L., Seshia, S. A., Abbeel, P., & Dragan, A. (2019). On the Utility of Learning about Humans for Human-AI Coordination. NeurIPS 2019. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.1910.05789

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: AH2AC2 Hanabi Human Dataset (Dizdarević et al. 2025)
prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: A dataset of 3,079 Hanabi games collected from human players, used to train the behavioral cloning (BC-LSTM) human proxy agent included in this evaluation set. The BC-LSTM agent weights (bc_2p.safetensors) were trained directly on this data.
prov:wasDerivedFrom.citation: Dizdarević, T., Hammond, R., Gessler, T., Calinescu, A., Cook, J., Gallici, M., Lupu, A., Muglich, D., Forkel, J., & Foerster, J. N. (2025). Ad-Hoc Human-AI Coordination Challenge. ICML 2025. https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.21490

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: RL Teammate Generation (LBF and Overcooked)
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: RL-based evaluation teammates for LBF and Overcooked-v1 were generated using the following algorithms: Fictitious Co-Play (FCP; Strouse et al. 2021), BRDiv (Rahman et al. 2022), LBRDiv (Rahman et al. 2024), CoMeDi (Sarkar et al. 2023), and Independent PPO (IPPO; Yu et al. 2022). Agents were trained in JAX using Jumanji (LBF) and JaxMARL (Overcooked-v1) environment implementations. Multiple seeds and checkpoints are included per algorithm and task to increase teammate diversity.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.name: Hanabi Agent Collection
prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Hanabi evaluation teammates include: (1) rule-based agents (IGGI, Cautious, Flawed, Internal, Outer, PIERS, SmartBot, Van den Bergh) implemented in Python; (2) a behavioral cloning LSTM agent (BC-LSTM) trained on the AH2AC2 human Hanabi dataset (Dizdarević et al. 2025); and (3) two Off-Belief Learning R2D2 agents (OBL-R2D2 at levels 1 and 4). No additional preprocessing or annotation was applied beyond agent training.
